zone_6b_notes: Plant bare-root crowns in early spring (late March to April) in Farmington. Everbearing (day-neutral) varieties produce fruit June through October — smaller, more continuous harvest than June-bearing. Better suited for containers, raised beds, or snacking harvests rather than bulk canning/preserving. Fewer runners than June-bearing; plant 12 inches apart in a hill system (remove all runners). Remove flowers for first 6 weeks after planting to build plant strength. Albion and Seascape are reliable day-neutral varieties. Less productive overall than June-bearing but extends fresh eating season through fall.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant bare-root crowns late March to April; hill system
